name: Roger Oliveira
title: AI Engineer
location: Porto Alegre, Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil
summary: I am an AI Engineer and software developer with experience since 2015 building products and internal platforms across both large companies and startup environments. My background combines full-stack engineering, distributed systems, micro frontends, and applied AI, with recent work centered on LLM-powered workflows, RAG pipelines, MCP servers, and agent-based developer tooling. I help teams apply stronger SDLC practices and approaches such as Spec-Driven Development to turn AI into a reliable engineering capability, reducing rework and improving delivery quality. I hold a degree in Systems Analysis and Development from FIAP, along with postgraduate studies in Data Science, Software Engineering, Mobile Development, and Leadership and People Management.

roles.title: AI Engineer
tasks: Build applied AI solutions that transform unstructured data into structured outputs using React.js, Python, FastAPI, and LLM families such as GPT, Claude, Mistral, and Qwen.

Help engineering teams apply stronger SDLC practices and approaches such as Spec-Driven Development, using AI with clearer specifications, review loops, and measurable outcomes instead of undisciplined workflows ("Vibe Coding").

Build agentic workflows and AI-enabled developer tooling with frameworks and SDKs such as Mastra, Agno, AI SDK, GitHub Copilot SDK, MCP SDK, and Streamlit, with emphasis on reliability, reviewability, and engineering discipline.

roles.title: Senior Developer
tasks: Delivered value as a full-stack engineer by modernizing the RUN HR module from PHP MVC to Node.js microservices and React.js micro frontends.
Contributed to a 634% increase in module adoption, 666% more engagement with new hire forms, more than 130K HR documents downloaded, and a 230% increase in Handbook publications.
Expanded the platform architecture with StencilJS-based micro frontends and Node.js services that supported broader product integrations, including Embedded Payroll.
Worked across product modernization, platform integration, and frontend architecture in a large-scale enterprise environment.

experience.title: Senior Developer
tasks: Acted as Technical Lead for BuzzFeed Brazil's website and CMS, owning delivery quality across the local platform.
Managed a team of five developers, including backlog prioritization, sprint planning, sprint reviews, and day-to-day delivery coordination.
Aligned development practices and product quality with BuzzFeed's global engineering team while coordinating with local partners and stakeholders.

experience.title: Senior Software Engineer
tasks: Designed and implemented .NET microservices for LATAM Finance IT systems with focus on scalability, performance, and business continuity.
Collaborated with teams across Panama, Colombia, Chile, and Argentina to support regional supply chain software requirements.
Delivered critical backend services in a short engagement, contributing quickly in a distributed enterprise environment.

experience.title: Software Engineer
tasks: Developed features for Rocky DEM, a desktop application focused on 3D particle simulation.
Worked across C++ core APIs and Python 3.6 with Qt in the graphical interface of a specialized engineering product.
Contributed to product evolution in a highly technical environment that required close collaboration between software and engineering domains.

roles.title: Software Engineer III
tasks: Served as technical lead on the Protrack3 project, defining the migration from a self-hosted monolith to a cloud-native microservices architecture.
Established stronger engineering quality practices by introducing backend unit tests and frontend behavior testing across the team.

roles.title: Software Engineer II
tasks: Maintained and evolved the Protrack2 ASP.NET application used to manage R&D hour reporting for the Brazilian Ministry of Science and Technology.
Built a financial management module for project expenses, invoices, and accounting records, extending the platform's operational scope.

roles.title: Software Engineer I
tasks: Maintained a legacy ASP.NET Web Forms application as part of the Operations team, ensuring stability in a production environment.
Supported software configuration management tasks including VM monitoring, repository administration, backups, and tape-based storage routines.

experience.title: Software Developer
tasks: Worked with supply chain and sales data using Microsoft Excel and Microsoft SQL Server to support business reporting and analysis.
Automated recurring workflows with Python and shell scripting, improving internal productivity and reducing manual effort.
Supported reporting and data quality initiatives in customer-facing business contexts.

experience.title: Technical Support Engineer
tasks: Provided technical support in a corporate banking environment.
Handled operational and end-user issues to help maintain day-to-day service continuity.

experience.title: Intern
tasks: Started professional experience as an intern supporting internal activities and operational routines.
Built foundational experience working inside an established public transportation organization.

certifications.name: Development of Modern and Scalable Applications with Microservices
Professional Qualification Certificate in Digital Entrepreneurship and IT Management
Professional Qualification Certificate in Mobile Application Development
Professional Qualification Certificate in System Analysis and Web Prototyping
Professional Qualification Certificate in Web 2.0 Developer and Designer

volunteering.description: Supported the community inside and outside HP by organizing workshops with psychologists and field specialists on history, gender expression, gender identity, and sexual and emotional orientation, while also coordinating donations for LGBTQI people at risk.

Visited the Amparo Santa Cruz Institution in Porto Alegre every two weeks to teach programming classes for adolescents from the local community, helping students build better life choices through technology.

opensource.description: Fixed an example in the JSDocs for the Expect.ObjectContaining in the testing module.
Translated some files and examples from English to Portuguese as part of a community effort.
